checks.a_analysis: The output contains significant content duplication. Almost all of the pricing data presented in the structured 'table' elements (Elements 0-11) is repeated in its entirety within the 'Pricing Continuation' section (Element 12) as a series of long 'paragraph' blocks. For example, the pricing for Part 159169 appears both in the 'ArcGIS Hub Premium Additional Prices' table and the first paragraph of the 'Pricing Continuation' section.
checks.b_evidence_source: The pricing entry for part '159169' appears only once on page 31 of the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: The entry '159169 ArcGIS Hub Premium Additional ArcGIS Online Credits $98.00 $118.00' appears in the table element (index 11) and again in the paragraph element (index 12, paragraph 1).
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'ArcGIS Hub Premium Additional Prices' and 'Pricing Continuation', elements 11 and 12

checks.a_analysis: The output uses a flat structure where each table is treated as a root-level section. While it ignores the high-level numeric groupings (e.g., Section 1, Section 2) present in the source PDF, it does not skip heading levels or nest them incorrectly according to the strict criteria. It is a normalized flat hierarchy.

The output fails to maintain the tabular structure for a large portion of the document. In the 'Pricing Continuation' section, dozens of pricing tables from the source PDF (pages 31-38) are rendered as raw text within 'paragraph' elements instead of 'table' elements. Additionally, the output injects eight 'Dummy' tables at the end of the 'Pricing Continuation' section which do not exist in the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The 'ArcGIS Image Dedicated Tiled' pricing data on page 32 is a structured table.
checks.b_evidence_output: The data is rendered as a text block: '165600 ArcGIS Image Dedicated Tiled Imagery Server Annual Subscription $16,000.00 $16,000.00...' and eight 'Dummy column' tables are appended.
checks.b_evidence_location: Section 'Pricing Continuation', element 12, paragraph 2 and subsequent dummy tables.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is fundamentally broken. The document logically begins on page 1, yet the output places the content from pages 1-21 into placeholder sections at the very end of the JSON (Elements 13 and 14). This moves the introduction and initial half of the price list after the conclusion, disrupting the logical flow of information.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document begins on Page 1 with 'ArcGIS Desktop and Extensions'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Placeholder sections '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' and '[Pages 11-21 unavailable]' appear as the final elements in the document sequence.

checks.a_analysis: The source document consists entirely of section headers and tables; there are no bulleted or numbered lists present to evaluate.
There are no links in the source PDF and no links were injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output introduces a massive 'Pricing Continuation' section at the end of the document which consists of a single, unformatted text dump of all the pricing data found in the preceding tables. This is a significant departure from the organized, tabular layout of the source PDF. While the primary content is correctly organized into tables earlier in the document, this massive block of unformatted text fundamentally changes the visual organization and flow, making the end of the document a 'wall of text' that does not match the source's intent for structured information. Additionally, the end of the document contains various technical artifacts like 'Tab 1 Price: ...' and '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' which further disrupt the professional layout of the government document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF maintains a consistent tabular layout across all 38 pages, with data categorized under specific headers (e.g., 'ArcGIS Enterprise - Perpetual License Prices' on page 6).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output includes a 'Pricing Continuation' heading followed by a massive unformatted text block containing the same data (e.g., 'ArcGIS Enterprise Advanced Up to Four Cores Perpetual License; 500 Viewers, 25 Creator N/A $40,802.00'). This block begins in screenshot 4 and continues through screenshot 5.
checks.b_evidence_location: End of document, starting with the 'Pricing Continuation' heading.

checks.a_analysis: The primary tables in the HTML output are rendered correctly with clear borders, distinct headers, and properly aligned columns. A sighted user can easily read and understand the data in these tables. While the 'Pricing Continuation' section renders tabular data as plain text, this is treated as a layout and duplication issue rather than a failure of the table rendering engine itself, as the primary tables are functional.
The source PDF is a text and table-heavy document with no meaningful images, logos, or charts. No broken image placeholders or missing essential visual content was found in the HTML output.

There is extreme content duplication in the HTML output. The entire price list data is presented twice: once in correctly formatted tables and a second time in the 'Pricing Continuation' text block at the end of the document. Furthermore, at the very bottom of the document, the phrase 'Tab [x] Price: ... Summary columns' is visually repeated multiple times (from Tab 1 through Tab 10), which appears to be a technical conversion artifact and does not exist in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Each price entry appears exactly once in the PDF (e.g., ArcGIS Enterprise Advanced... on page 6).
checks.b_evidence_output: The price entry for 'ArcGIS Enterprise Advanced... $40,802.00' appears once in a table and again in the 'Pricing Continuation' block. The text 'Tab 1 Price: ... Summary columns' is followed immediately by 'Tab 2 Price: ... Summary columns' and so on, repeated ten times.
checks.b_evidence_location: Screenshots 4 and 5, 'Pricing Continuation' section and the very bottom of the document.
